arts.ttl: Nigeria boycott Libya match after players ‘locked in airport without food or drink for 12 hours’
arts.sum: Nigeria were due to play an African Cup of Nations qualifier against Libya on Tuesday but have pulled out after ‘disgraceful’ treatment

arts.ttl: Nigeria boycott fixture, with government called in to rescue team after 'disgraceful behaviour'
arts.sum: Nigeria are refusing to play their AFCON qualification fixture against Libya on Tuesday night, after the national team players claim they have spent more than 12 hours in an abandoned airport with no phone connection, food or water. After beating Libya in Uyo on Friday night, winning 1-0, Nigeria were then due to play the return fixture in Libya on Tuesday evening.

arts.ttl: Osimhen calls it a ‘hostage situation’ as Nigeria left stranded in Libya
arts.sum: Ademola Lookman, Samuel Chukwueze, Fisayo Dele-Bashiru and the rest of Nigeria squad have been left...

arts.ttl: Osimhen slams 'INHUMANE' treatment of Nigeria team-mates
arts.sum: Victor Osimhen has called out the Confederation of African Football (CAF) after Nigeria's squad were left stranded at Libya airport ahead of their Africa Cup of Nations qualifier. Osimhen posted on Instagram Stories : “I am disappointed by the unfair treatment my brothers and coaches are facing at the Libya airport last night.

arts.ttl: Preview: Libya vs. Nigeria - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Tuesday's Africa Cup of Nations Qualifying clash between Libya and Nigeria, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.
